literature_thresholds.description: Prompt for retrieving literature-based CBC thresholds for a disease via LLM
literature_thresholds.template:
You are a clinical hematology expert. For {disease_full}, provide CBC biomarker thresholds used in clinical practice to help identify or screen for the disease.

Return ONLY a JSON object. Keys are CBC feature names from this list: rdw, hgb, hct, wbc, plt, mcv, mch, mchc, rbc.
Each value is an object with:
  "threshold": numeric value (float)
  "direction": "above" (high value indicates disease) or "below" (low value indicates disease)
  "source": one-sentence clinical rationale

Omit features with no relevant threshold for {disease_full}.
Return ONLY the JSON object, no explanation, no markdown.

cbc_features.description: Detailed descriptions of CBC features and their RA relevance

hct.name: Hematocrit
hct.description: Percentage of red blood cells in total blood volume
hct.ra_relevance: Often decreased in RA due to anemia of chronic disease

hgb.name: Hemoglobin
hgb.description: Oxygen-carrying protein in red blood cells
hgb.ra_relevance: Reduced in anemia of chronic inflammation common in RA

mch.name: Mean Corpuscular Hemoglobin
mch.description: Average amount of hemoglobin per red blood cell
mch.ra_relevance: May be reduced in iron-deficiency anemia secondary to RA

mchc.name: Mean Corpuscular Hemoglobin Concentration
mchc.description: Average concentration of hemoglobin in red blood cells
mchc.ra_relevance: Decreased in hypochromic anemias associated with RA

mcv.name: Mean Corpuscular Volume
mcv.description: Average size/volume of red blood cells
mcv.ra_relevance: Normocytic or microcytic pattern common in RA anemia

plt.name: Platelets
plt.description: Cell fragments involved in clotting and inflammation
plt.ra_relevance: Reactive thrombocytosis is a well-documented marker of active RA

rbc.name: Red Blood Cell Count
rbc.description: Number of red blood cells per volume of blood
rbc.ra_relevance: Decreased count observed in RA-associated anemia

rdw.name: Red Cell Distribution Width
rdw.description: Variation in red blood cell size (anisocytosis)
rdw.ra_relevance: Elevated RDW is a systemic inflammation marker linked to RA severity

wbc.name: White Blood Cell Count
wbc.description: Total count of immune cells in blood
wbc.ra_relevance: Can be elevated in active RA inflammation or lowered by immunosuppressive treatment

neut_pct.name: Neutrophil percentage
neut_pct.description: Percentage of white blood cells that are neutrophils
neut_pct.ra_relevance: Elevated in acute inflammation; NLR (neut_pct/lym_pct) is a validated inflammation marker

lym_pct.name: Lymphocyte percentage
lym_pct.description: Percentage of white blood cells that are lymphocytes
lym_pct.ra_relevance: Key adaptive immune cell; often reduced relative to neutrophils in RA (elevated NLR)

mono_pct.name: Monocyte percentage
mono_pct.description: Percentage of white blood cells that are monocytes
mono_pct.ra_relevance: Innate immunity; elevated in chronic inflammation and autoimmune disease

eos_pct.name: Eosinophil percentage
eos_pct.description: Percentage of white blood cells that are eosinophils
eos_pct.ra_relevance: Allergic/parasitic response; may be altered in autoimmune conditions

baso_pct.name: Basophil percentage
baso_pct.description: Percentage of white blood cells that are basophils
baso_pct.ra_relevance: Rare; elevated in hypersensitivity and some autoimmune conditions

format_spec.description: Output format specification for LLM-generated formulas
format_spec.template:
OUTPUT FORMAT (strict):
Return exactly {n_formulas} formula(s), one per line, using this syntax:
  FORMULA: <python expression>

Rules:
- Use only these variable names: hct, hgb, mch, mchc, mcv, plt, rbc, rdw, wbc, neut_pct, lym_pct, mono_pct, eos_pct, baso_pct
- Note: neut_pct + lym_pct + mono_pct + eos_pct + baso_pct ≈ 100 (they are parts of the same differential)
- Allowed operators: +  -  *  /  **
- Allowed functions: np.sqrt()  np.log1p()  np.abs()
- No conditionals, no loops, no external symbols
- Each formula must be a single expression (no assignment, no def)
- Use 3 to 7 features per formula
- Higher output should correspond to higher probability of Rheumatoid Arthritis

Examples (style only, do not reuse):
  FORMULA: rdw * plt / (hgb + 0.01)
  FORMULA: np.log1p(neut_pct / (lym_pct + 0.01)) * rdw
  FORMULA: (neut_pct - lym_pct) / (neut_pct + lym_pct + 0.01) * plt

cot_instruction.description: Chain-of-thought instruction for reasoning before formula generation
cot_instruction.template:
Before each formula, briefly explain (2–3 sentences) the clinical rationale
for the combination you chose. Label each block:
  REASONING: <your reasoning>
  FORMULA: <python expression>

blind.description: Prompt that relies solely on clinical/medical knowledge without data-driven hints
notes: cot_section is either empty string or COT_INSTRUCTION based on chain_of_thought parameter
format_spec is filled with n_formulas value

seeded.description: Prompt seeded with data-driven insights from prior experiments
seeded.template:
You are a clinical data scientist with deep expertise in hematology and autoimmune disease. Your task is to design mathematical biomarker formulas that can identify patients likely to have Rheumatoid Arthritis (RA) using Complete Blood Count (CBC) measurements alone.

CLINICAL CONTEXT:
Rheumatoid Arthritis is a systemic autoimmune disease causing chronic joint inflammation. It commonly induces anemia of chronic disease, reactive thrombocytosis, and elevated markers of red cell heterogeneity. CBC panels are routinely collected and may carry subtle RA-related signals.

AVAILABLE CBC FEATURES:
{feature_block}

DATA-DRIVEN GUIDANCE (derived from prior GP experiments on this dataset):
1. Feature importance ranking (most to least discriminative):
   {top_features}

2. Effective transformations observed on this heavily imbalanced dataset (~1% positive rate):
   - np.log1p() on skewed count features (e.g. plt, wbc, rbc) reduces outlier influence
   - np.sqrt() on ratio features helps compress variance
   - Ratios between features (e.g. rdw / mcv, neut_pct / lym_pct) can amplify subtle group differences

3. Optimal formula complexity: 3–7 features per formula. Simpler formulas
   generalise better; overly complex formulas tend to overfit.

TASK:
Using the above clinical knowledge and data-driven guidance, propose {n_formulas} distinct formula(s) that compute a continuous score where higher values indicate a greater likelihood of RA. Aim for diverse combinations — avoid repeating the same feature pairings.
{cot_section}
{format_spec}
notes: cot_section is either empty string or COT_INSTRUCTION based on chain_of_thought parameter
format_spec is filled with n_formulas value
Contains hardcoded feature importance ranking and transformation guidance based on experiments

configurations.description: Standard prompt configurations used in method4_llm.py
notes: Seeded strategy: includes feature importance and transformation guidance

metadata.description: Centralized prompt library for the RA biomarker pipeline
usage_notes: All prompts use MedGemma 4B IT model (google/medgemma-4b-it)
Method 1 prompts query for clinical literature thresholds
Method 4 prompts generate mathematical biomarker formulas
See source files for implementation details
